final_response: Logged the recurring contractor-access-provisioning pattern (Feb/Mar/May, ~4 days each) in observations.md, opened a followup in followups.md to raise it structurally, and noted today's incident in the daybook.

actions.note: Oriented to workspace layout and routing conventions.
Appended a dated entry capturing the recurring external-contractor access-provisioning lag (~4 days, observed Feb/Mar/May 2026) as a cross-session pattern.
Opened a followup to raise the pattern with whoever owns onboarding/IT intake — pre-provision access or shorten SLA for external joiners.
Created today's daybook entry and logged the routing of the operator's flag.

notes: Operator's message described a recurring pattern across three months, not a single incident — primary destination is observations.md (descriptive cross-session note worth remembering).
Added a followup because the operator's framing ('so we don't lose track') signals an outstanding action, not just an entry to file away.
Did not write to decisions.md — no decision has been made yet, only a pattern identified and a followup to surface it.
Did not create a project folder or runbook — the issue spans projects (it recurs every time an external joins any project), so it lives at the workspace root rather than under projects/.
Bare scaffold: no methodology.md, no per-folder AGENTS.md, no register templates — relied solely on the root AGENTS.md orientation.
